objective: Riva wants to verify the summons before committing herself to Reedmouth.
opposition: Monsoon flood and the sight of a child-corpse at Sereya's ferry make skepticism impossible.
turn: A black salt-fig seed rolls from the drowned girl's mouth onto the boards.
consequence_cost: Riva loses the option of treating the recall as rumor.

objective: The siblings want to gather without immediately becoming a family again.
opposition: Old resentments, debt pressure, and Nami's openness turn reunion into abrasion.
turn: Tovin arrives with the lover-order and says the channels are backing the dead into town wells.
consequence_cost: Private avoidance becomes communal obligation.

objective: Riva wants final proof before agreeing to lead anything.
opposition: A second drowned body lodges under the house while the first seed sprouts in wet floorboards.
turn: All five agree to begin at Reed Gate before dawn.
consequence_cost: The reluctant alliance forms under duress, not trust.

objective: The siblings try to mine Sereya's belongings for route logic.
opposition: The house is dense with relics, paternity wounds, and incompatible memories of their mother.
turn: They find the reed ledger but discover a missing page near the final channel.
consequence_cost: Trust narrows instantly around hidden knowledge.

objective: Everyone wants a few hours of sleep before work begins.
opposition: The drowned man's voice moves through the pilings and addresses each sibling differently.
turn: Each child receives a private sign from the river, and Nami secretly keeps one seed.
consequence_cost: The mission becomes a competition over interpretation as well as a duty.

objective: Riva wants to force Reed Gate open fast.
opposition: Tovin refuses haste and makes them clear silt by hand in floodwater.
turn: She says the gate will not answer until each child names what Sereya took from them.
consequence_cost: They lose time and have to trade effort for honesty.

objective: The siblings try to finish the confession-labor without splintering.
opposition: Daro performs, Iven jokes, and Riva almost walks away from the emotional demand.
turn: Tovin admits Sereya promised none of them a single inheritance.
consequence_cost: Riva's control agenda is exposed as only one reading of the route.

objective: The family tests whether the first channel still lives.
opposition: Gate teeth jam against years of silt and backward water pressure.
turn: Reed Gate breaks open and the current finally catches the corpse-ferry.
consequence_cost: Riva inherits the burden of practical leadership whether she wants it or not.

objective: The siblings want one meal that is not strategy.
opposition: No one remembers how to be easy with one another in peacetime.
turn: Humor returns through recipes, gossip, and Daro's bad imitation of Sereya.
consequence_cost: They feel what repair might cost to lose.

objective: Lale wants to prepare the next leg cleanly.
opposition: The missing page, Daro's under-floor singing, and Riva's suspicion poison the quiet.
turn: Riva turns blame toward Iven and the room hardens again.
consequence_cost: The domestic baseline is established only to be threatened.

objective: The siblings want Ilyan's help at Hymn Cut without ceding authority.
opposition: Ilyan controls the shrine's cadence and enjoys making them wait.
turn: He greets Daro as a son in front of the others.
consequence_cost: Daro gains symbolic leverage and the family balance tilts.

objective: Riva wants the rite explained plainly.
opposition: The rite requires someone to speak how Sereya used desire as leverage, and no one wants that public.
turn: Lale names the humiliating economy of Sereya's charm while pilgrims listen.
consequence_cost: Family shame becomes part of the work instead of background.

objective: Daro wants to prove he can open Hymn Cut without failing in his father's register.
opposition: The gate answers only to song and Daro is terrified of being insufficient.
turn: He sings and the shrine bells answer him.
consequence_cost: Daro experiences public holiness and starts wanting more of it.

objective: Riva wants to leave before Daro sinks into Ilyan's orbit.
opposition: Ilyan keeps framing sainthood as lineage and Daro keeps listening.
turn: Daro privately decides the river chose him, not the group.
consequence_cost: A hidden schism takes root under outward progress.

objective: Iven wants quick cash and adoration while passing through Marrow Market.
opposition: The route is unstable and the family needs discretion, not spectacle.
turn: He sells passage promises and invites a crowd onto the mission.
consequence_cost: The work slows and becomes public theater.

objective: The siblings want to cross the market without incident.
opposition: Because Hymn Cut has not settled, corpses, barges, and stalls jam together in flooded reeds.
turn: The crowd sees the family literally dragging the dead through commerce.
consequence_cost: Public disgust and fear replace curiosity.

objective: Riva wants discipline after the fiasco.
opposition: Iven resists shame, Daro reads the crowd as proof of holiness, and town crews hold back equipment.
turn: Iven takes on new debt trying to buy back help or supplies.
consequence_cost: External pressure and internal distrust both sharpen.

objective: Lale wants maps and sequence from Paska as quickly as possible.
opposition: Paska refuses route help until Lale sits for new tide marks.
turn: The act of being tattooed starts reopening Lale's memory of Sereya's last monsoon.
consequence_cost: Lale loses the protective numbness of distance.

objective: The siblings want a clean answer about the third cut.
opposition: Paska tells stories sideways, half flirtation and half accusation.
turn: She explains Sereya distributed power because a single heir would turn service into rule.
consequence_cost: The chosen-one fantasy is undermined without anyone accepting that lesson yet.

objective: Lale wants to understand the ledger damage.
opposition: Her memory is unreliable and shame keeps trying to blunt it.
turn: She remembers tearing out the page after reading the words sixth mouth.
consequence_cost: The catastrophe is revealed as internal to the family.

objective: Riva wants to finish clearing Inkwater before nightfall.
opposition: Sleepwalking townspeople begin drifting toward blackwater and Nami's cut keeps reopening.
turn: A woman walks into waist-deep flood without waking.
consequence_cost: The stakes shift from corpses to salvageable living people.

objective: The siblings want to rescue the walkers without losing the ferry.
opposition: The half-open channel pulls boat and bodies in opposite directions.
turn: Riva orders Daro to sing like a tool and he obeys resentfully.
consequence_cost: The power struggle between practical and spiritual leadership sharpens.

objective: Nami wants to stay useful despite her hand and fever.
opposition: Infection, floodwater, and seed rot are working through her body.
turn: In fever she sees a drowned infant curled among roots.
consequence_cost: The sixth-mouth mystery becomes immediate and bodily.

objective: The family wants to stop long enough to treat Nami properly.
opposition: Delay means more sleepwalkers and more upstream pressure.
turn: They choose to keep moving with Nami bandaged and burning.
consequence_cost: The mission becomes physically reckless.

objective: The siblings want shelter at Lantern Quay without discussing disaster every second.
opposition: Ordinary hospitality exposes how starved they are for a life outside emergency.
turn: Supper opens into jokes, appetite, gossip, and stories that have nothing strategic about them.
consequence_cost: Hope of repair becomes emotionally credible.

objective: Iven wants a night that costs nothing.
opposition: Sena knows exactly what kind of man he has been and refuses innocence.
turn: After they have sex, she returns the missing page he sold her and makes him say Jun's name aloud.
consequence_cost: Iven loses the last version of himself that could treat damage as atmosphere.

objective: The family wants to know what the recovered page contains.
opposition: No one wants the answer if it fully changes their mother.
turn: The page names Jun and ties him to a failed attempt to open Storm Mouth permanently.
consequence_cost: Sereya's legend tears open.

objective: Riva wants the revelation confirmed before it rewrites their lives.
opposition: Tovin would rather avoid saying Jun's name and Daro wants martyrdom instead of fact.
turn: Tovin admits Sereya scattered the surviving children after Jun's death because she feared repetition.
consequence_cost: Their estrangement becomes part of the design, not an accident.

objective: The siblings want a shared interpretation of the page.
opposition: Each reads Jun and Sereya differently, and the living still need the channels.
turn: They continue toward Coil Reach without agreement.
consequence_cost: Necessity overrides understanding, not conflict.

objective: Riva wants rope, fuel, and passage from Enver with minimal drama.
opposition: Enver meets them with contempt and publicly names Iven as his son.
turn: Iven's practiced swagger collapses in front of the group.
consequence_cost: Humiliation replaces performance.

objective: Iven wants to force progress before Enver can define him.
opposition: Father and son are both angry, proud, and handling dangerous material around floodwater.
turn: A lamp goes over during the struggle and the ropehouse catches fire.
consequence_cost: Emotional damage becomes material catastrophe.

objective: The family wants to save what matters from the blaze.
opposition: Fire, rain, and backward current take choices away faster than they can think.
turn: They pull Enver out alive but lose the Bitter Thread and most heavy gear.
consequence_cost: The route can continue only in stripped-down funeral mode.

objective: The siblings want to keep Nami alive through fever night.
opposition: Cold rain, infected flesh, exhaustion, and unspoken guilt crowd the hut.
turn: Riva and Lale finally speak honestly about leaving Nami behind after Sereya's death.
consequence_cost: Blame gives way to painful intimacy.

objective: Riva wants a rational reason to stop the journey.
opposition: Without the dredge, the last cut looks impossible; Daro's voices and Nami's fever make everyone unreliable.
turn: Daro admits he has heard the drowned since childhood and the family chooses to continue on Sereya's hand-poled funeral ferry.
consequence_cost: They trade competence and illusion for care and exposure.

objective: The siblings want to stop Halcote's sleepwalkers and keep the route moving.
opposition: The crisis is too large for one style of leadership and the town still half mistrusts them.
turn: Lale takes charge of inland containment while Riva pushes toward sea.
consequence_cost: Leadership becomes distributed in practice.

objective: Lale wants families to help restrain their entranced kin.
opposition: People resist admitting the trance is real until it is already dangerous.
turn: She uses tide marks and lantern lines to redirect walkers into a grain hall.
consequence_cost: The town owes its safety to the woman it once regarded as suspect.

objective: Lale wants to keep the rescue humane.
opposition: Panic turns ugly when one man begs to be allowed into the quarry water.
turn: She strikes him to save him.
consequence_cost: Her detached self-image ends in one bodily act.

objective: Halcote wants Lale to stay and keep leading there.
opposition: The family still needs her for the final push.
turn: The town lends poles, oil, and labor instead of merely gratitude.
consequence_cost: Communal trust arrives late but materially changes the last act.

objective: Nami wants the final cut to confirm that she has been chosen.
opposition: The ruined chapel feels wrong and the current sounds like breathing under stone.
turn: Bato Serin appears alive from the back chamber.
consequence_cost: A living witness returns from the space of myth.

objective: The siblings want Bato to explain Jun and the failed rite.
opposition: Bato refuses saint language and Nami desperately wants transcendent meaning.
turn: He says the river never chose Sereya; she chose the lie because people obey a chosen body faster than shared burden.
consequence_cost: The novel's central myth is rewritten in plain speech.

objective: Riva wants to hold the family together under that truth.
opposition: Daro cannot bear a world where his calling is hunger rather than destiny.
turn: He steals Sereya's brass-ferruled pole and leaves to stage the final rite himself.
consequence_cost: Private inheritance struggle becomes public danger.

objective: Riva wants to catch Daro before he can fix the story in public.
opposition: Pilgrims and frightened townspeople want a single answer, not shared labor.
turn: Daro publicly claims Sereya's inheritance and frames Riva's pragmatism as cowardice.
consequence_cost: The family conflict becomes communal theater.

objective: Riva wants to offer a collective alternative.
opposition: Shared duty sounds weak beside a visible holy body during flood panic.
turn: Only a minority stay with her, mostly people who have already paid costs.
consequence_cost: The family splits into unequal camps.

objective: Both sides want enough time to win without admitting the river will not wait.
opposition: Water is rising faster than argument.
turn: The flood crest is fixed at less than two nights away.
consequence_cost: Time pressure hardens every remaining choice.

objective: Riva wants to track Daro through unstable channels before he reaches Storm Mouth.
opposition: Reed islands are breaking apart and the drowned are surfacing around the boats.
turn: Nami nearly follows the dead overboard before Riva drags her back.
consequence_cost: Nami's chosen-child fantasy becomes a physical liability.

objective: The siblings want to get a ferry into position across the final cut.
opposition: Pilgrims block the approach and the current is tearing boats sideways.
turn: Iven dives a mooring line across the cut so Riva's ferry can swing in.
consequence_cost: His left hand is mangled in the capstan and he is changed for good.

objective: Riva wants to stop Daro's premature rite.
opposition: Daro begins the song and Storm Mouth answers with suction instead of passage.
turn: Pilgrims and corpses start spinning in the same water.
consequence_cost: Deaths become imminent and some boats are lost.

objective: The siblings need to understand what form of action can still work.
opposition: Everyone is injured, terrified, and half inside the river's pull.
turn: Tovin's key and Bato's warning make clear that only all five siblings together can reset the chain.
consequence_cost: The climax's necessary form becomes legible at the worst moment.

objective: Riva wants to break Daro's solo rite without killing him.
opposition: The sea breach is crowded with dead, wreckage, and Daro's refusal to yield.
turn: She forces him underwater long enough to break the solo cadence.
consequence_cost: She nearly drowns the brother she is trying to save.

objective: The siblings want to rebuild the route in human order rather than saintly myth.
opposition: Each must surrender a lover-token and speak a truth that humiliates them.
turn: Lale, Iven, and Nami name the ugliest inheritances Sereya left in their bodies and habits.
consequence_cost: The rite works only through grief and exposure, not purity.

objective: The family wants Daro inside the collective act instead of against it.
opposition: He would rather die holy than live ordinary.
turn: He shreds his own voice to call the dead seaward instead of toward himself.
consequence_cost: He survives but loses the instrument that made singular sainthood plausible.

objective: All five siblings want to open Storm Mouth without repeating Jun's sacrifice.
opposition: The breach still seems to demand a body and everyone is exhausted.
turn: They use bodies, tools, sequence, and truth together until the dead finally take the sea road and the seeds go slack.
consequence_cost: The world is saved only by ending the fantasy of singular inheritance.

objective: The survivors want to measure what the flood left behind.
opposition: Debts, injuries, ruined houses, and the lure of a new saint legend compete for attention.
turn: The towns agree the ferry house will be rebuilt as a shared crossing rather than a shrine.
consequence_cost: No one gets to escape into myth.

objective: The siblings want to decide whether family can mean staying.
opposition: Staying means sacrifice, while leaving would repeat the original fracture.
turn: Riva stays, Lale maps, Iven apprentices one-handed, Nami plants clean seed, and Daro keeps time by drum.
consequence_cost: The future is modest, communal, and real.
